questions.question: Prove that vertically opposite angles are equal when two lines intersect.
questions.answer: When two lines intersect, adjacent angles form linear pairs summing to 180°. Using this relation, vertically opposite angles can be shown equal.

questions.question: Explain the proof of the linear pair axiom.
questions.answer: When a ray stands on a straight line, it divides the straight angle into two adjacent angles whose sum equals 180°.

questions.question: Explain how corresponding angles prove that two lines are parallel.
questions.answer: If corresponding angles formed by a transversal are equal, then the lines are parallel.

questions.question: Explain the converse of alternate interior angles theorem.
questions.answer: If alternate interior angles are equal, the lines cut by the transversal are parallel.

questions.question: Explain interior angles on the same side of a transversal and prove they are supplementary.
questions.answer: Interior angles on the same side sum to 180° when lines are parallel.

questions.question: Explain how angles around a point form a complete angle.
questions.answer: All angles around a point sum to 360° which forms a complete angle.

questions.question: Explain the relationship between adjacent angles and linear pairs.
questions.answer: Linear pairs are special adjacent angles forming a straight line.

questions.question: Explain the significance of transversals in geometry.
questions.answer: Transversals help identify angle relationships and prove parallel lines.

questions.question: Explain how angle relationships help determine parallel lines.
questions.answer: Equal corresponding or alternate interior angles indicate parallel lines.

questions.question: Explain the concept of reflex angles and their role in geometry.
questions.answer: Reflex angles are angles greater than 180° used to represent larger rotations.

questions.question: Explain the concept of a straight angle and its geometric importance.
questions.answer: A straight angle measures 180° and represents a straight line.

questions.question: Explain the role of angles in geometric constructions.
questions.answer: Angles are used to construct triangles, polygons, and geometric figures.

questions.question: Explain the importance of angle relationships in solving geometry problems.
questions.answer: Angle relationships simplify calculations and help prove geometric properties.

questions.question: Explain how complementary and supplementary angles are used in geometry.
questions.answer: They help identify right and straight angle relationships.

questions.question: Explain the properties of intersecting lines.
questions.answer: Intersecting lines form vertically opposite angles and linear pairs.

questions.question: Explain how parallel lines maintain constant distance.
questions.answer: Parallel lines never intersect and remain equally spaced.

questions.question: Explain angle measurement using protractors.
questions.answer: Angles are measured in degrees using a protractor aligned with the vertex and arms.

questions.question: Explain the role of angles in polygon properties.
questions.answer: Angles determine polygon shapes and interior angle sums.

questions.question: Explain how angles help determine geometric symmetry.
questions.answer: Equal angles indicate symmetrical structures.

questions.question: Explain the concept of angle bisectors.
questions.answer: An angle bisector divides an angle into two equal parts.

questions.question: Explain the difference between adjacent and vertically opposite angles.
questions.answer: Adjacent angles share a side while vertically opposite angles lie opposite each other.

questions.question: Explain the difference between acute and obtuse angles.
questions.answer: Acute angles are less than 90° while obtuse angles lie between 90° and 180°.

questions.question: Explain the relationship between right angles and perpendicular lines.
questions.answer: Perpendicular lines intersect forming four right angles.

questions.question: Explain the geometric importance of 360° angle measurement.
questions.answer: 360° represents a complete rotation around a point.

questions.question: Explain the importance of angle relationships in architecture and engineering.
questions.answer: Angles help design stable structures and accurate measurements in engineering and construction.
